revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:埴輪の日]]
'''Haniwa Day''' (埴輪の日) is a commemorative day celebrating the [[Kofun period]]’s {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} (clay figures) to promote regional {{ill|History|en|History|qid=Q309|lt=History|1=History}} and {{ill|Culture|en|Culture|qid=Q11042|lt=Culture|1=Culture}}. Two main dates are observed: August 20 and August 28. One official observance was established by a company in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and recognized by the {{ill|Japan Anniversary Association|ja|日本の記念日一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Anniversary Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記念日|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記念日|1=List of Japanese anniversaries and memorial days|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11506089}} (detailed below). Additionally, other {{ill|local governments|ja|地方公共団体|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/local governments|1=local public entity|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q27116296}} and historical museums have independently set or recognized their own "Haniwa Days," with varying names like "Haniwa Day" (ハニワの日) or "Haniwa no Hi" (はにわの日). == Overview == The dates are derived from wordplay (goroawase) on the Japanese pronunciation of "haniwa." August 20 represents "Ha (8) - Ni (2) - Wa (0)," while August 28 represents "Ha (8) - Ni (2) - Wa (8)." Regions with prominent [[Kofun cluster|Kofun clusters]] or rich findings of figurative haniwa, such as {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}, and {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|qid=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, have adopted these dates for [[community revitalization]] or educational purposes. In some cases, the term is used temporarily for a specific event rather than an ongoing tradition. == Examples == Local governments, companies, and historical museums that observe "Haniwa Day" often hold history- and archaeology-themed events, such as haniwa-making workshops or {{ill|Magatama|en|Magatama|qid=Q334232|lt=magatama|1=Magatama}} crafting experiences. * In {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|qid=Q694308|lt=Habikino|1=Habikino}}, Osaka Prefecture, home to the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, the Okura Printing Industry Co., Ltd., which operates the "Kawachi Konda Haniwa-no-Sato Okuraya" facility, established August 28 as "Kawachi Konda Haniwa Day." As of August 2020, it remains the only "Haniwa Day" recognized and registered by the Japan Anniversary Association.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kinenbi.gr.jp/|title=Kawachi Konda Haniwa Day|work=Japan Anniversary Association|accessdate=2020-08-28|qq=}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=http://okura-hd.jp/release/200806.html|title=Haniwa-making Experience Facility "Kawachi Konda Haniwa-no-Sato Okuraya" Establishes 'Kawachi Konda Haniwa Day' on August 28|work=Okura Printing Industry Co., Ltd.|accessdate=2020-08-06|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Sakai|en|Sakai|qid=Q193428|lt=Sakai|1=Sakai}}, Osaka Prefecture, home to the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|qid=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}}, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, the city declared August 28, 2019, as "Haniwa Day." On this day, the city’s World Heritage PR mascot "Haniwa Kachō" was promoted to "Haniwa Buchō, CHO (Chief Haniwa Officer)."<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/shisei/koho/citypromo/haniwa/index.html|title=Welcome, I’m Haniwa Buchō, CHO (Chief Haniwa Officer)|work=Sakai City Public Relations Strategy Division|accessdate=2020-08-28|qq=}}</ref>{{Refnest|group="note"|Haniwa Buchō was later promoted to "Haniwa Special Assignment Manager" on August 26, 2020, and received an official appointment certificate dated August 28.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.mbs.jp/news/sp/kansainews/20200826/GE00034548.shtml|title=Haniwa Buchō Becomes 'Special Assignment Manager' for Overall PR in Sakai City|newspaper=[[Mainichi Broadcasting System]]|date=2020-08-26|accessdate=2020-08-28|qq=}}</ref>|qq=}} * In {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|qid=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki|1=Takatsuki}}, Osaka Prefecture, where the {{ill|Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja|三島野古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishima Kofun Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三島古墳群|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三島古墳群}} is located, August 20 was designated as "Haniwa Day" to promote the city's history and attractions. The city’s mascot "Hanitan," based on a {{ill|armed clay figure|ja|挂甲武人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/armed clay figure|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} excavated from {{ill|Imashirozuka Kofun|en|Imashirozuka Kofun|qid=Q11376795|lt=Imashirozuka Kofun|1=Imashirozuka Kofun}}, was officially recognized on June 8, 2011, and its birthday is celebrated on August 20.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/m/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/daio/kodairekishikan/kanchou/kochikan11_20/1351471315882.html|title=Exploring the 'Hanitan' Mascot in Archaeology|work=Takatsuki City Cultural Properties Division|accessdate=2020-08-28|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Shibayama, Chiba|en|Shibayama, Chiba|qid=Q1203689|lt=Shibayama|1=Shibayama, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture, located near the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}}{{Refnest|group="note"|The Shibayama Kofun Cluster is located in the neighboring town of {{ill|Yokoshibahikari|en|Yokoshibahikari|qid=Q1203260|lt=Yokoshibahikari|1=Yokoshibahikari}}.|qq=}}, the municipal {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|en|Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|qid=Q11614636|1=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|lt=Shibayama Kofun and Haniwa Museum|qid=Q11614636|lt=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum}} observes August 28 as "Haniwa Day." On this day in 2019, the museum offered free admission and activities like magatama crafting, though no events were announced for 2020.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.haniwakan.com/pr/topics.html|title=News & Updates|work=Shibayama Kofun Haniwa Museum|accessdate=2020-08-28|qq=}}</ref> * In {{ill|Takasaki|en|Takasaki|qid=Q336438|lt=Takasaki|1=Takasaki}}, Gunma Prefecture, known for large kofun clusters like the [[Watanuki Kannonyama Kofun]], the {{ill|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|en|Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|qid=Q20982941|lt=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History|1=Gunma Prefectural Museum of History}} celebrated both August 20 and 28 as "Haniwa Day" in 2019 with commemorative events.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://mainichi.jp/articles/20190814/k00/00m/040/047000c|title=Gunma Prefectural History Museum Holds Events for 'Haniwa Day' on August 20 and 28|newspaper={{ill|Mainichi Shimbun|en|Mainichi Shimbun|qid=Q1136866|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|1=Mainichi Shimbun}}|date=2019-08-14|accessdate=2020-08-28|qq=}}</ref> == Notes == {{Reflist|group="note"|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == *{{Cite book|author=Shibayama Town|title=Shibayama Tourism Guide: Furari Shibayama|chapter=Seasonal Events|publisher=Shibayama Town Development Division and Tourism Association|page=18|year=2016|date=2016-07|url=http://www.town.shibayama.lg.jp/cmsfiles/contents/0000002/2458/furari-shibayama.japanese.pdf|ref=harv|qq=}}
== See Also == * [[Kofun]] * {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} * [[Commemorative Days]] == External Links == * [https://www.kinenbi.gr.jp/ Japan Anniversary Association]
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|埴輪の日|version=97956961|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:埴輪の日]]
